content: A curiosity-driven autonomous agent can gate its own memory consolidation via a meta-learning objective that prioritizes retaining unexpected, high-curiosity experiences over familiar low-surprise ones, creating a self-improving memory system that grows more aligned with the agent's intrinsic exploration goals over time. This framework resolves the common problem of catastrophic forgetting in diverse open-ended exploration by only consolidating surprising information that drives future curiosity, rather than retaining all encountered data.
